title: Leptocallisia multiflora
taxonomy: Leptocallisia multiflora is classified within the family Cucurbitaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as gourds or cucumbers. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other members of the Cucurbitaceae family. Leptocallisia multiflora is further grouped within the tribe Cucurbiteae, which is a sub-group of the Cucurbitaceae family. The genus Leptocallisia is also part of this classification, and it is a small group of plants that are closely related to the genus Cucumis. The exact classification of Leptocallisia multiflora within the Cucurbitaceae family can be further refined using more advanced techniques such as DNA sequencing.

intro: Leptocallisia multiflora, also known as creeping eclipta, is a small, flowering plant native to tropical regions, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of creeping eclipta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, creeping eclipta is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to treat a range of health issues, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and wounds.
